title: Integer compositions for Juhl formulas
summary: Defines ordered compositions I=(I₁,…,Iᵣ) of N with positive parts and provides the empty, singleton, and two-part constructors.

mathematicalContent.heading: Ordered compositions
mathematicalContent.body: A composition of N is a finite ordered list of positive integers whose sum is N. Order matters because the associated GJMS operators are composed in that order.

mathematicalContent.heading: Basic constructors
mathematicalContent.body: The empty composition belongs to N=0; for positive N there is a singleton (N), and positive a,b give the ordered pair (a,b).

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores an ordered list of positive integers whose sum is N.
Defines the number of parts of a composition.
Constructs the unique empty composition of zero.

correspondence.leanType: def singleton (N : Nat) (hN : 0 < N) : Composition N
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Constructs the one-part composition (N) when N is positive.
Unfolds the parts list of the empty composition.

correspondence.leanType: theorem singleton_parts (N : Nat) (hN : 0 < N) : (singleton N hN).parts = [N]
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Unfolds the parts list of the singleton composition.

correspondence.leanType: theorem singleton_length (N : Nat) (hN : 0 < N) : (singleton N hN).length = 1
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves that a singleton composition has length one.

correspondence.leanType: def pair (a b : Nat) (ha : 0 < a) (hb : 0 < b) : Composition (a + b)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Constructs the ordered two-part composition (a,b) from positive a and b.

correspondence.leanType: theorem pair_parts (a b : Nat) (ha : 0 < a) (hb : 0 < b) : (pair a b ha hb).parts = [a, b]
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Unfolds the parts list of the pair composition.

references.note: Source for ordered GJMS compositions and coefficients.

references.locator: Direct ambient proof of Juhl's formulas
references.note: Ambient interpretation of the composition formula.

limitations: This file supplies the combinatorial carrier for Juhl formulas only. It does not enumerate all compositions as a finite set, define Juhl coefficients, or prove any operator identity.
